description: Security group sg-0084 (sg-custom-0084) allows inbound PostgreSQL (port 5432) from 0.0.0.0/0
remediation: Restrict port 5432 (PostgreSQL) to specific trusted IP ranges. Use a bastion host or VPN rather than direct public access. Apply least-privilege inbound rules.

description: Security group sg-0048 (sg-custom-0048) allows inbound RDP (port 3389) from 0.0.0.0/0
remediation: Restrict port 3389 (RDP) to specific trusted IP ranges. Use a bastion host or VPN rather than direct public access. Apply least-privilege inbound rules.

description: Security group sg-0077 (sg-custom-0077) allows inbound Redis (port 6379) from 0.0.0.0/0
remediation: Restrict port 6379 (Redis) to specific trusted IP ranges. Use a bastion host or VPN rather than direct public access. Apply least-privilege inbound rules.

description: Security group sg-0008 (sg-custom-0008) allows ALL inbound traffic from the internet (protocol=-1, 0.0.0.0/0)
remediation: Remove the catch-all inbound rule immediately. Replace with explicit, minimal rules for each required port/service.

description: Security group sg-0006 (sg-custom-0006) allows inbound PostgreSQL (port 5432) from 0.0.0.0/0
remediation: Restrict port 5432 (PostgreSQL) to specific trusted IP ranges. Use a bastion host or VPN rather than direct public access. Apply least-privilege inbound rules.

remediation: Enable "Block all public access" in the S3 console, set bucket ACL to private, and review bucket policies. Immediately audit data exposure given PII flag.
